title: Paid in Beer: How Egyptians Built the Pyramids
script: This sounds fake, but the builders of the pyramids went on strike — and won. Around 1170 BC, the workers at Deir el-Medina downed tools and marched on the nearest temple, demanding their overdue wages: rations of bread, beer, and onions. It's the oldest recorded labor strike in human history. But here's the twist — these weren't slaves. They were paid, skilled state employees with documented contracts and sick days. Half of everything you were taught about pyramid builders was wrong.
search_summary: Ancient Egyptian workers at Deir el-Medina staged history's first recorded labor strike around 1170 BC demanding overdue rations.
comment_question: Still think the pyramids were built by slaves? 👀
source_note: Oriental Institute, University of Chicago; Egyptologist Pierre Grandet
